vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry indicates P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) should be present. Visual evidence confirms both pots are devoid of viable plant matter, containing only soil and debris. P1 and P3 remain, though P3 shows signs of severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: Soil surface appears undisturbed; no visible growth.
plant_audit.P2: Empty. No signs of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remaining. Shows signs of chlorosis and structural weakening.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2; no plant matter present.

biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in P3/P4 area. Electronic component (header pin) observed on desk surface in T-3 and T-2, removed by T-1.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Removal of receipt/paper debris from desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Removal of electronic component from desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant drop in ambient light; potential sensor or lighting adjustment.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Restoration of lighting; plant in P3 shows continued postural collapse.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical. The single leaf is drooping significantly, indicating a failure in turgor pressure and likely root system collapse.

anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P3/P4 pot (likely an attempt at calcium supplementation or pH adjustment).
Electronic component (header pin) on desk surface (T-3, T-2).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of advanced decline. P2 and P4 are effectively lost, showing no signs of life. P3 is the only remaining specimen with visible foliage, but it is in a terminal state of collapse. The presence of eggshells suggests an intervention that has failed to arrest the decline. The environment is currently hostile to the remaining specimen.
